patch.value: GH-503: flattened extension-command aliases (csv-export, jira-sync) drop option contracts

Auto-generated flattened top-level aliases for extension command groups (pm csv-export export, pm jira-sync sync) do not inherit the option contracts of the canonical nested form (pm csv export). Two agent-hostile defects: (1) flattened alias --help renders ZERO option lines while the canonical form lists all 12 (metadata exists in pm contracts --json); (2) flattened alias silently IGNORES unknown options (pm csv-export export --bogus runs; canonical form correctly rejects) — a mistyped/hallucinated flag silently drops, so an agent believes an option took effect (e.g. --output silently ignored, output goes to stdout only). Framework-level: reproduced with two independent extensions (pm-csv, pm-jira) whose metadata IS declared; the gap is in how the CLI/SDK synthesizes the <group>-<sub> alias — it registers the command name but not the canonical command's option definitions. pm --help advertises the flattened form as a top-level entry, so it is the natural agent discovery path.

Flattened <group>-<sub> aliases inherit the canonical command's full option contract; --help on the flattened form renders the same option lines as the canonical form; unknown options on the flattened form fail fast identically to the canonical form; regression test covers both defects with a fixture extension; GH-503 closed with a comment linking the fix
